docs: add LIMITATIONS.md - honest design boundaries and layered defense (#953)

Addresses valid external critique of AGT's architectural blind spots:

1. Action vs Intent: AGT governs individual actions, not reasoning or
   action sequences. Documents the compound-action gap explicitly and
   recommends content policies + model safety layers.

2. Audit logs record attempts, not outcomes: Documents that post-action
   state verification is the user's responsibility today, with hooks planned.

3. Performance honesty: README now notes that <0.1ms is policy-eval only;
   distributed mesh adds 5-50ms. Full breakdown in LIMITATIONS.md.

4. Complexity spectrum: Documents the minimal path (just PolicyEvaluator,
   no mesh/crypto) vs full enterprise stack.

5. Vendor independence: Documents zero cloud dependencies in core,
   standard formats for all state, migration path.

6. Recommended layered defense architecture diagram showing AGT as one
   layer alongside model safety, application logic, and infrastructure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(mcp): centralize constants, add error sanitization, decompose mcp-server

Address six review findings from the qsv-agent-skills code review:

- Centralize duplicate magic numbers (50MB output, 1MB stderr, 5s timeout)
  into tool-constants.ts; update all consumers
- Add sanitizeErrorForClient() to strip absolute paths from MCP error
  responses; apply at 5 errorResult() call sites in tool-handlers.ts
- Guard against ReDoS in handleSearchToolsCall by rejecting regex
  patterns exceeding 200 chars
- Extract WorkingDirManager class (316 lines) from mcp-server.ts,
  reducing it from 1616 to 1308 lines (-19%)
- Add 44 unit tests for file-operations.ts (path resolution, auto-index,
  temp files, param aliases, conversion args)
- Add 26 unit tests for command-guidance.ts (guidance map, parameter
  enhancement, description enrichment with emoji conventions)

commit.message:
Renderer: enforce 2-tile scroll margin at visible viewport edges

The player could walk to the very edge of the CSS-visible window and
continue scrolling with no surrounding context. Add SCROLL_MARGIN = 2:
the viewport now shifts to keep the player at least 2 tiles inset from
each visible edge, so surrounding walls/tiles are always shown.
When the map boundary prevents centring, the margin relaxes gracefully
(void tiles appear on the opposite side rather than losing the player).

invalidation (from mutation onSuccess) has completed before the test
proceeds to check card visibility.

Root cause: React Query v5 mutateAsync resolves before onSuccess
callbacks complete, creating a race between dialog closure and
cache invalidation.

visible when the upload method returns.

commit.message:
Fix mobile: keep player visible when viewport clamps at map edge

Cover-mode CSS scaling clips the canvas on one axis. On portrait mobile
the canvas overflows horizontally, so only the centre ~29 of 60 columns
are visible. When the player walked to a map edge, the map-bounds clamp
(vpX = 0) pushed the player into the hidden left portion of the canvas.

- Renderer: track visibleCols/visibleRows (set via setVisibleViewport)
- Renderer.draw: two-step viewport calc — clamp to map bounds first,
  then shift to guarantee player stays within the visible CSS window
  (may render void tiles at the far edge rather than losing the player)
- Game: expose setRendererViewport() to forward values to the renderer
- main.ts: fitCanvas now returns visibleCols/visibleRows (computed from
  availW/availH divided by tile size × scale) and wires them to game

commit.message:
feat: event-driven Now Playing with MISSED grace period (#175)

* fix: countdown timer flicker and accuracy

Server now renders 'Ns' directly when ≤30s remaining instead of
'less than a minute', eliminating the flip-flop between server-rendered
text and JS countdown. JS tick() runs immediately on page load instead
of waiting 1s for the first setInterval callback.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: dashboard live countdown and seconds in activity timestamps

Replace full page reload with in-place DOM updates for the Now Playing
countdown timer. The 5s poll now syncs remaining_seconds from the API
and a 1s ticker interpolates between polls, so the countdown never
resets. Structural changes (new schedules, device status, activity)
still trigger a full reload.

Add seconds to Recent Activity and History timestamps so short clips
no longer appear to start and end at the same time.

Bump version to 1.17.5.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: recompute remaining_seconds fresh in API to prevent countdown jumps

The scheduler tick runs every 15s, so remaining_seconds in _now_playing
can be up to 15s stale. The dashboard JS polls every 5s and syncs from
this value, causing the countdown to jump back to a higher number.

Fix: store start_time_raw and end_time_raw in _now_playing, then
recompute remaining_seconds at request time in dashboard_json()
using the current server time. This gives the JS countdown a fresh
baseline on every poll.

Also bumps version to 1.17.6.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: fresh remaining_seconds on page render + reduce reload triggers

Two root causes of countdown jumping:

1. Initial page render used stale remaining_seconds from the scheduler
   (15s tick). Now recomputes fresh in both dashboard() and
   dashboard_json().

2. Structural fingerprint was too sensitive — activity_count and
   starting/mismatch status flags caused unnecessary page reloads
   that reset the JS countdown. Removed transient fields from the
   fingerprint so reloads only happen for truly structural changes
   (different schedules, device list, pending/orphaned/offline).

Also adds a past-time-window warning when saving a schedule whose
end date is today but end time has already passed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add seconds to data-utc timestamp formatter in app.js

The client-side JS in app.js overwrites all [data-utc] elements with
toLocaleTimeString using only hour+minute, stripping the seconds that
the server-side Jinja2 template renders. Adding second: '2-digit' to
the formatter options fixes the display.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore starting/mismatch status in structural fingerprint

The starting and mismatch flags need to trigger reloads so the
badge transitions are visible (Starting -> Playing, mismatch
detection). These cause at most one reload per transition, unlike
activity_count which caused repeated reloads.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: match number input height to time input height

Both input[type=time] and input[type=number] now use 0.6rem vertical
padding so loop count and priority fields align with start/end time.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: event-driven Now Playing with MISSED grace period

Refactor Now Playing from scheduler-driven to device event-driven:

Protocol:
- Add PLAYBACK_STARTED/PLAYBACK_ENDED message types and Pydantic models

WebSocket handler:
- Handle PLAYBACK_STARTED: look up schedule, build now_playing entry, log STARTED
- Handle PLAYBACK_ENDED: clear now_playing, log ENDED

Scheduler:
- Remove _now_playing management and STARTED/ENDED logging
- Add 60s grace period before logging MISSED (prevents false positives from
  brief network hiccups)
- Track _offline_since per (schedule_id, device_id) combo
- Clean up _now_playing for disconnected devices
- Export set_now_playing/clear_now_playing/log_schedule_event as public API

Schedule deletion:
- Clear _now_playing entries immediately when schedule deleted

Tests:
- Rewrite TestNowPlayingCleanup for event-driven model (5 tests)
- Rewrite test_now_playing_has_remaining_seconds as set_now_playing test
- Add TestMissedGracePeriod (3 tests: before grace, after grace, reconnect)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: bump version to 1.18.0

* fix: e2e test uses 23:59 end_time to avoid CI time-of-day failure

The TestScheduleEditWithDates._setup_schedule used end_time 17:00.
When CI runs after 17:00 UTC, 'today at 17:00' is in the past, so the
'in the past' warning modal correctly appears and the test fails.
Change to 23:59 so the schedule is almost always in the future.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: re-run HIGH-priority experiments on corrected data

Re-ran 4 experiments on post-ODC-fix data (corrected percentage temp basals):

EXP-2431 (Model Performance): AUC stable (0.803 hypo, 0.901 hyper).
  LOPO matches colleague (0.67 vs 0.67). No change in conclusions.

EXP-2451 (Basal Debate): AAPS basalIOB mean dropped to 0.746 U
  (vs Loop 1.581 U). 12/19 supply-demand too high. Conclusions hold
  but AAPS-specific numbers are now physiologically plausible.

EXP-2461 (IOB Protective): 13/19 patients protective (was ~all).
  Some ODC patients now show RR>1 (odc-58680324: 2.322,
  odc-61403732: 1.538). Overall RR=0.692 still strongly protective.
  Nuance: protection is population-level, not universal.

EXP-2491 (Cross-Algorithm): Loop↔AAPS transfer symmetric
  (0.637 vs 0.644). Feature importance ρ=0.808 between algorithms.
  2/3 findings algorithm-agnostic. Transfer works across algorithms.

All reports updated with ✅ post-fix provenance. 16 figures refreshed.
